# Research: entry-validation-ui-patterns

*Auto-generated by AIDLC planning session*

**Question:** What UI patterns (preview + confirm, inline validation, slot-based pickers) are most effective for preventing invalid pool entries before submission? Are there open-source golf or sports pick UI components worth referencing?

---

# Entry Validation UI Patterns for Golf Pool Picks

## Overview

Preventing invalid submissions in a golf pool app requires layering three complementary strategies: **real-time inline validation**, **slot-based pickers that constrain choices structurally**, and **preview-confirm flows** that surface errors before they reach the server. The right combination depends on pick complexity (e.g., salary cap pools vs. simple top-5 picks) and the moment of highest user error risk.

---

## 1. Inline Validation Patterns

### 1.1 Eager vs. Deferred Validation

| Strategy | When to Validate | Best For |
|---|---|---|
| **On-blur** | When user leaves a field/slot | Name inputs, manual text entry |
| **On-change** | Every keystroke or selection change | Dropdowns, toggles, numeric inputs |
| **On-submit-attempt** | When user first tries to submit | Catching untouched empty slots |
| **Hybrid** | Deferred until first blur, then eager | Complex multi-step forms |

For golf pools, **on-change validation** works best for slot fills (a golfer becomes visually "locked in" immediately) while **on-submit-attempt** catches empty slots the user hasn't touched.

### 1.2 Visual Error States

**Don't use red alone.** Colorblind users (8% of males) miss pure red/green signaling. Layer:
- Icon (✓ / ✗ / ⚠)
- Text label ("Already picked", "Golfer missed cut")
- Border/background tint (not color-only)
- ARIA `aria-invalid="true"` + `aria-describedby` pointing to error message

```html
<!-- Accessible error slot -->
<div class="pick-slot pick-slot--error" role="group" aria-labelledby="slot-3-label">
  <label id="slot-3-label">Pick #3</label>
  <div class="golfer-badge golfer-badge--invalid" aria-invalid="true" aria-describedby="slot-3-error">
    Rory McIlroy
  </div>
  <p id="slot-3-error" class="error-message" role="alert">
    ⚠ Rory McIlroy is already selected in Pick #1
  </p>
</div>
```

### 1.3 Duplicate Detection

Duplicate picks are the #1 invalid-submission type in pool apps. Implement at the selection layer, not just on submit:

```javascript
// React pattern: validate on each pick change
const isDuplicate = (golfer, currentSlotIndex, allPicks) =>
  allPicks.some((pick, i) => i !== currentSlotIndex && pick?.id === golfer.id);

// Disable already-chosen golfers in the picker dropdown
const availableGolfers = golfers.map(g => ({
  ...g,
  disabled: picks.some(p => p?.id === g.id),
  disabledReason: 'Already selected'
}));
```

### 1.4 Salary Cap / Group Constraint Validation

For tiered/salary-cap formats, show a running tally that updates live:

```
[Tier Budget Tracker]
Tier A: 1/1 used  ████████████ $12,500 / $12,500
Tier B: 2/2 used  ████████████ $18,000 / $18,000
Tier C: 1/2 used  ██████░░░░░░ $6,000 / $12,000  ← 1 pick remaining
Tier D: 0/1 used  ░░░░░░░░░░░░ $0 / $8,000       ← Required
```

The visual tracker is more effective than a text error because users understand budget exhaustion spatially.

---

## 2. Slot-Based Picker Patterns

### 2.1 Structural Constraint (Best Pattern)

The most effective validation is **making invalid states unrepresentable in the UI**. Slot-based pickers that enforce one-golfer-per-slot, with golfers removed from the available list once picked, eliminate whole categories of errors before validation is needed.

```
[Pick Sheet]

Slot 1: [ Scottie Scheffler ▾ ] ✓
Slot 2: [ Rory McIlroy      ▾ ] ✓
Slot 3: [ Select a golfer   ▾ ]   ← Opens filtered dropdown
Slot 4: [ Select a golfer   ▾ ]
Slot 5: [ Select a golfer   ▾ ]

Available golfers in dropdown for Slot 3:
  ✓ Xander Schauffele
  ✓ Collin Morikawa
  ✗ Scottie Scheffler (already picked)  ← grayed out or hidden
  ✗ Rory McIlroy (already picked)
```

**Decision: hide or gray out?**
- **Gray out + tooltip** when the pool has few golfers or users need to see who's taken
- **Hide entirely** for large fields (150+ golfers) where showing disabled entries adds noise

### 2.2 Drag-and-Drop Ranking Pickers

For ranked picks (predict top-5 finishing order), DnD pickers work well. Key validation points:

- Enforce minimum slots filled before "Save" activates
- Show rank numbers (1st, 2nd, etc.) next to each slot, not just position in list
- Allow click-to-swap (not just drag) for mobile users
- Ghost placeholder should show target rank label during drag

**Libraries:**
- `@dnd-kit/core` (React) — most accessible DnD library currently, good touch support
- `react-beautiful-dnd` — battle-tested but in maintenance mode (Atlassian)
- `Sortable.js` — framework-agnostic, very performant, less accessible

### 2.3 Bracket/Grid Pickers

For Ryder Cup or match-play pools where users pick winners per match, a grid layout with radio-button-style binary choices is cleaner than dropdowns:

```
[Match 1]  ○ USA   ●  EUROPE  ← Selected

[Match 2]  ○ USA   ○  EUROPE  ← Required, not yet selected  [!]

[Match 3]  ●  USA   ○  EUROPE
```

Required matches with no selection should be flagged inline, not just on submit.

### 2.4 Progressive Disclosure

For pools with conditional rules (e.g., "Pick a tiebreaker golfer only if your top 5 are all in the same tier"), show optional/conditional slots only after prerequisites are met:

```
[Required Picks 1–5] ← All filled ✓

[Tiebreaker Pick]    ← Appears after all 5 required slots are filled
  This pick only counts if two entries have identical scores.
  [ Select tiebreaker golfer ▾ ]
```

This avoids confusing empty-slot errors for fields users don't know they need.

---

## 3. Preview + Confirm Flows

### 3.1 When to Use a Preview Step

A dedicated preview screen pays off when:
- The entry has more than 6–8 picks
- Rules are complex (tiers, salary caps, scoring multipliers)
- The deadline is hard (no edits after cutoff)
- First-time users are likely (Masters pool with casual participants)

Skip the preview step for simple 3-pick or weekly DFS-lite pools — it adds friction without value.

### 3.2 Preview Screen Anatomy

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════╗
║  YOUR MASTERS POOL ENTRY             ║
║  Mike F. · 2026 Masters              ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════╣
║  PICK #  GOLFER             TIER     ║
║  1       Scottie Scheffler  A  $12.5K║
║  2       Rory McIlroy       A  —     ║ ← Error: Two Tier A picks
║  3       Collin Morikawa    B  $9.2K ║
║  4       Xander Schauffele  B  $8.8K ║
║  5       Viktor Hovland     C  $6.1K ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════╣
║  ⚠ ISSUES FOUND (1)                  ║
║  • You have 2 Tier A picks. Max is 1.║
║    Fix → [Edit Entry]                ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Tiebreaker: Tommy Fleetwood         ║
║  Deadline: Thursday Apr 9, noon ET   ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════╣
║  [← Edit Entry]    [Submit Entry →]  ║ ← Submit disabled if errors
╚══════════════════════════════════════╝
```

**Key rules:**
1. **Disable the submit button** when validation errors exist — don't just warn
2. Show errors in the preview, not just in a toast
3. The "Edit Entry" CTA should deep-link back to the specific problematic slot
4. Show the deadline prominently — deadline anxiety is a major UX driver

### 3.3 Optimistic Confirm with Server Re-Validation

After the user submits, server-side re-validation must still run (user's session could have expired, pool could have closed). Pattern:

```
1. Client validates → all clear → preview shown
2. User clicks "Submit"
3. Show loading spinner on button
4. POST /api/entries
5a. Server 200 → show confirmation screen with entry ID
5b. Server 422 → return to form with server error inline:
    "This golfer became ineligible after the cut was updated.
     Please replace Pick #3."
```

Never show a generic "Something went wrong" on validation failures — always surface the specific field.

### 3.4 Confirmation Screen

After successful submission:

```
✓ Entry Submitted!

Entry ID: #4821
Submitted: Sunday, Apr 6 at 11:47 AM ET

Your picks:
  1. Scottie Scheffler
  2. Collin Morikawa
  3. Xander Schauffele
  4. Viktor Hovland
  5. Tommy Fleetwood

[View Leaderboard]  [Edit Entry]  [Share Entry]
```

Show the "Edit Entry" button here too — pool commissioners almost always allow edits until the deadline, and users frequently want to change a pick after submission. Clarify the deadline again.

---

## 4. Form-Level Error Summary Pattern

When a user hits "Submit" with multiple errors, don't just highlight the first error — show an **error summary** at the top of the form:

```
⚠ Please fix 3 issues before submitting:

  • Pick #2 is empty — select a golfer
  • Pick #4 (Jon Rahm) is already selected in Pick #1
  • Tiebreaker pick is required

[Jump to Pick #2 ↓]  [Jump to Pick #4 ↓]  [Jump to Tiebreaker ↓]
```

This pattern (from GOV.UK Design System) significantly reduces frustration for users who can't see the inline errors due to scrolling past them. The jump links scroll to and focus the problematic field.

---

## 5. Mobile-Specific Considerations

Golf pools are often filled on mobile, frequently in the days before a tournament. Mobile validation patterns differ from desktop:

### 5.1 Bottom Sheet Pickers

Replace dropdowns with bottom sheets on mobile:

```
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ Select Pick #3              │
│ ─────────────────────────── │
│ 🔍 Search golfers...        │
│                             │
│ Available                   │
│ ● Collin Morikawa   $9.2K  │
│ ● Xander Schauffele $8.8K  │
│ ● Viktor Hovland    $7.4K  │
│                             │
│ Already Picked (grayed)     │
│ ○ Scottie Scheffler         │
│ ○ Rory McIlroy              │
└─────────────────────────────┘
```

Bottom sheets give more touch area than native `<select>` elements and allow search/filter.

### 5.2 Sticky Submit Bar

On mobile, keep a sticky bottom bar that shows completion status and the submit button:

```
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  3 / 5 picks complete       │
│  [Submit Entry]  (disabled) │
└─────────────────────────────┘
```

This constantly reinforces how many picks remain without the user needing to scroll up.

### 5.3 Haptic Feedback

On native/PWA contexts, use haptic feedback:
- Light tap: golfer selected successfully
- Error vibration pattern: duplicate detected
- Success vibration: form submitted

---

## 6. Open-Source Components Worth Referencing

### 6.1 General Form Validation

| Library | URL | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **React Hook Form** | react-hook-form.com | Best DX for React; built-in `mode: "onChange"` for live validation |
| **Zod** | zod.dev | Schema-first validation; share client/server validation logic |
| **Formik** | formik.org | Older but stable; large ecosystem |
| **VeeValidate** | vee-validate.logaretm.com | Vue equivalent |

**Recommended pattern:** React Hook Form + Zod resolver, with a shared schema that runs identically on the API route.

### 6.2 Combobox / Golfer Pickers

| Component | URL | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Radix UI Select** | radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select | Unstyled, fully accessible |
| **Headless UI Combobox** | headlessui.com | Tailwind-friendly, solid keyboard nav |
| **Downshift** | github.com/downshift-js/downshift | Low-level hooks for custom comboboxes |
| **react-select** | react-select.com | Feature-rich; virtualization available via `react-window` |
| **Tanstack Virtual** | tanstack.com/virtual | Virtualizes long golfer lists (150+) |

For a list of 150+ golfers, **virtualization is essential** — rendering all options tanks mobile performance.

### 6.3 Drag and Drop (for Ranked Picks)

| Library | URL | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **@dnd-kit/core** | dndkit.com | Best accessibility, touch support, React |
| **Sortable.js** | sortablejs.com | Framework-agnostic, fast |
| **react-beautiful-dnd** | github.com/atlassian/react-beautiful-dnd | Maintenance mode, still widely used |

### 6.4 Sports/Golf UI References

**No mature open-source golf pick UI components exist** as of early 2026, but these are the closest references:

| Project | URL | What to Borrow |
|---|---|---|
| **ESPN Tournament Challenge (open patterns)** | — | Bracket lock-in UX; watch the network tab |
| **DraftKings DFS UI** | — | Salary cap budget tracker pattern |
| **Yahoo Fantasy Sports** | — | Draft board with available/taken player states |
| **Sleeper App** | sleeper.com | Mobile-first draft UX; bottom sheet pickers |
| **Underdog Fantasy** | — | Pick'em card UI; tap-to-select pattern |

Study these as reference via browser DevTools/inspection, not source code.

**Open source sports-adjacent:**
- `react-bracket` (GitHub: g-loot/react-bracket) — tournament bracket rendering
- `sport-stats-charts` — scoreboard-style data tables

### 6.5 Error Summary Components

- GOV.UK Design System error summary: `design-system.service.gov.uk/components/error-summary/` — the canonical implementation of accessible error summaries, worth porting
- Reach UI (reach.tech) — accessible dialog and disclosure patterns for confirmation modals

---

## 7. Validation Rule Taxonomy for Golf Pools

A reference list of every validation rule that commonly appears in golf pool apps, to inform your Zod schema design:

### Hard Rules (Block Submission)
- Minimum picks not met (e.g., must have exactly 5)
- Duplicate golfer across picks
- Golfer not in tournament field
- Golfer already withdrew or WD
- Tier/group limit exceeded (e.g., max 1 Tier A pick)
- Salary cap exceeded
- Deadline passed

### Soft Rules (Warn, Don't Block)
- Tiebreaker not selected (if optional)
- All picks from same country (unusual, likely unintentional)
- High-owned golfer in a contrarian pool (informational)

### Post-Cutoff Validation (Server Only)
- Golfer missed cut (if pool locks before cut)
- Golfer disqualified

---

## 8. Recommended Architecture for This Project

Given that this is a React + Vite app (based on repo context), the recommended stack:

```
Schema Layer:     Zod schemas (shared between client + API)
Form State:       React Hook Form with zodResolver
Golfer Picker:    Headless UI Combobox + Tanstack Virtual (for 150+ field)
DnD (if ranked):  @dnd-kit/core
Preview Step:     Separate route (/entry/preview) — makes "back" work correctly
Confirm Dialog:   Radix UI AlertDialog — accessible keyboard trap
Error Summary:    Custom component following GOV.UK pattern
Mobile Pickers:   Radix UI Dialog as bottom sheet (via CSS positioning)
```

**Key architectural decision:** Put the Zod schema in a shared `lib/validation/entrySchema.ts` file imported by both the React form and the API route handler. This guarantees client and server validation never drift apart — the #1 source of "passed client validation but failed server" bugs.

---

## Summary: Priority Order

1. **Structural constraints first** — slot-based pickers that make duplicates impossible
2. **Live budget/tier tracker** — visual feedback as picks are made
3. **On-change inline validation** — immediate error on duplicate or invalid selection
4. **Error summary on submit attempt** — jump links to every broken field
5. **Preview screen** — for complex pools or first-time users
6. **Server re-validation with field-specific errors** — always, no exceptions
7. **Confirmation screen with deadline + edit option** — reduce post-submit anxiety
